title.purpose: Saved-item titles and compact row hierarchy.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary navigation or reversible action button.

components.name: Command Search
components.description: Primary search/operator shell for fuzzy search and command entry.

components.description: Applied operator or status filter chip with removable/toggle behavior.

components.name: Saved Item Row
components.description: Core unit of triage: source, title, metadata, tags, media, and F/A/D actions in one scan path.

Example saved item title that can be scanned quickly

components.css: .ds-row{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:.75rem;padding:.75rem 1rem;border:1px solid var(--border-hairline,#2a2f3a);border-radius:14px;background:var(--surface-card,#171a21);color:var(--text-body,#e0e5ec);box-shadow:var(--shadow-row,0 1px 0 rgba(0,0,0,.22));font-family:Lexend,system-ui,sans-serif}.ds-avatar{display:grid;place-items:center;width:32px;height:32px;border-radius:999px;background:var(--surface-inset,#20242d);color:var(--accent,#f2a97e);font:700 .75rem/1 JetBrains Mono,ui-monospace,monospace}.ds-row-main{min-width:0;flex:1}.ds-row h3{margin:0;color:var(--text-body,#e0e5ec);font:600 .97rem/1.32 Lexend,system-ui,sans-serif}.ds-row p{margin:.2rem 0 0;color:var(--text-muted,#98a2b3);font:400 .85rem/1.4 Lexend,system-ui,sans-serif}.ds-row span{font:600 .73rem/1 JetBrains Mono,ui-monospace,monospace}.ds-monitor{display:grid;place-items:center;width:96px;height:54px;border-radius:8px;background:var(--surface-inset,#20242d);color:var(--text-dim,#636e7e);font:600 .73rem/1 JetBrains Mono,ui-monospace,monospace}

components.name: Cleared Empty State
components.description: Permission-oriented empty state for focus batches and completed pages.
components.html:
✓

Page cleared

Nothing carries over. Draw another batch or stop here.
components.css: .ds-empty{max-width:36rem;margin:2rem auto;padding:1.5rem;border:1px solid var(--border-hairline,#2a2f3a);border-radius:20px;background:var(--surface-card,#171a21);color:var(--text-body,#e0e5ec);text-align:center;font-family:Lexend,system-ui,sans-serif}.ds-empty div{display:grid;place-items:center;width:44px;height:44px;margin:0 auto .75rem;border-radius:999px;background:var(--archive-tint,rgba(84,199,148,.13));color:var(--status-archive,#54c794);font-weight:700}.ds-empty h2{margin:0;font:700 1.45rem/1.26 Lexend,system-ui,sans-serif}.ds-empty p{margin:.5rem 0 0;color:var(--text-muted,#98a2b3);font:400 .97rem/1.55 Lexend,system-ui,sans-serif}

narrative.overview: content-hoarder should feel like a personal operations log for reducing a messy backlog: dense enough to move quickly, calm enough to keep the user from bouncing, and tactile enough that each decision feels confirmed. The v3 browse surface is the primary expression: a dark “night ops” default with a daylight alternate, Lexend for human-readable scanning, JetBrains Mono for instrument readouts, fixed action colors, and source badges that identify content without turning the app into confetti. The system rejects generic SaaS polish, decorative hero language, and any UI that celebrates hoarding more content. Visual decisions serve search, filtering, triage, media review, recovery, and error recovery. Empty states should feel like permission to stop; error states should be specific and non-blaming.
keyCharacteristics: Dense, legible list-first layouts with optional card/pinboard density, not endless nested cards.
Restrained dark-first palette with one warm phosphor action accent and a stable status triad.
Tactile controls: pills, pebbles, command bars, row actions, and sheet panels with short state transitions.
Media previews and source glyphs that help decide quickly without stealing the whole hierarchy.
Copy that reduces guilt: “Page cleared”, “That’s enough for today”, “nothing carries over”.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: On v3 browse, apricot phosphor is the only product accent. Keep / Archive / Done colors are semantic states, and source hues are identity marks. Do not invent decorative accents.

rules.name: The Semantic Status Rule
rules.body: Keep is blue, Archive is green, Done is red. Do not remap these by page, density, or mood.

rules.name: The No Panic Rule
rules.body: Irreversible or failure states can be clear and red-adjacent, but backlog volume and empty/error copy must not become alarmist.

rules.name: The Scan First Rule
rules.body: Titles, metadata, duration, tags, and source must be distinguishable in one glance. If the user has to reread a row to find the decision signal, the hierarchy failed.

rules.name: The Product Type Rule
rules.body: No display fonts, ornamental serifs, or huge marketing headers in task surfaces. This is a tool, not a landing page.

rules.name: The Ledger, Not Cards Rule
rules.body: Use rows, sheets, rails, and tonal groups before reaching for card stacks. Nested cards are prohibited.

rules.name: The Shadow Has a Job Rule
rules.body: A shadow means overlay, focus, or separation. If a shadow is only decorative, remove it.

dos: Do preserve the dark-first “night ops” identity for v3 browse and the current teal legacy identity until those pages are intentionally migrated.
Do use semantic aliases from `static/core/tokens.css` for v3 browse components and `static/tokens.css` for legacy `/triage` + `/reddit` pages.
Do keep Keep / Archive / Done colors fixed and visible across keyboard, pointer, swipe, and touch paths.
Do write empty states that reduce pressure and offer a next action: another batch, enough for today, adjust filters, or recover context.
Do make destructive or irreversible flows explicit, scoped, and backed by confirmation / backup language.
Do verify mobile/PWA behavior when changing browse, triage, media, drawer, dock, swipe, or install-related UI.
donts: Don't use generic SaaS dashboards with decorative metrics, hero gradients, and marketing-page polish.
Don't create a hoarding-machine UI that celebrates accumulating more instead of clearing or deciding.
Don't use guilt-driven productivity copy: streak shame, overdue language, red-alert backlog panic, or scolding empty states.
Don't use overstimulating neon terminal aesthetics, glassmorphism, ornamental animations, or decorative card grids.
Don't make ambiguous destructive actions around deletes, Reddit unsave, recovery, archival, or purge flows.
Don't escape trusted source glyph SVG output from `chIcon()`; visible SVG text is a source-badge regression.
Don't dedupe `static/core/tokens.css` and `static/tokens.css`; both are intentional until legacy pages migrate.
